title: alchemilla palmata subsp. subcrenata
intro: Alchemilla palmata subsp. subcrenata, also known as the alpine lady's mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Alchemilla. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which make it useful in treating various health conditions, including wounds, burns, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe digestive problems. Alchemilla palmata subsp. subcrenata has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including eczema, acne, and other skin conditions, as well as to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
